observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting entries to properly account the stale checks in accordance with the provisions of Section 59 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account.
observations.reason: (See Finding No. 7)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
direct the MDRRMC to: (a) prepare and submit a supplemental/revised LDRRMFIP
to be approved by the Sangguniang Bayan and the LCE for all new projects to be
funded from the DRRM unexpended balances and include under a separate caption,
the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous
year and include projects to be funded by the supplemental budget in conformity with
the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002; and (b) strictly use the 30% Quick
Response Fund (QRF) in accordance with Section 22 of RA 10121 and Section 6.3 of
the DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-001.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Development
Council (LDC) and the Municipal Planning and Development Council as the LDC
secretariat prepare an approved Supplemental or Amended AIP for newly identified
and qualified PPAs that will be sourced out of the 20% DF to be submitted to the
Sangguniang Bayan for adoption and approval.

We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed that the Local
Chief Executive require the (a) Inventory Team (i) conduct complete annual physical
inventory-taking of PPE; and (ii) submit a complete RPCPPE not later than January
31 of each year; and (b) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, in
coordination with the other department heads, exert joint efforts to create a complete
set of property records, property cards and ledger cards for each type of PPE and,
henceforth, ensure that they are complete and regularly updated.

We also recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief
Executive designate a dedicated property officer, if the budget allows, to ensure that
all functions pertaining to supply and property management are carried out properly.

We reiterated and Management agreed that the Management (a) exhaust
possible means to secure copies of the TCT of the lots and buildings owned by the
Municipality and submit to the Audit Team for verification; and (b) designate a
personnel to work on documentation for the legality of ownership of the lots together
with the structures built thereon; and (c) appropriate additional funds to cover the
cost of transfer of titles.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
direct the Municipal General Services Officer to (a) prepare the IIRUP to include all
unserviceable properties as basis in recording the de-recognition thereof from the
books of accounts; and (b) facilitate immediate disposal of the said unserviceable
properties pursuant to Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to (a) require the Property Office to
renew all PARs that reached the prescribed period of three years and come up with a
systematic filing of the documents preferably in alphabetical sequence for easy access
and reference; and (b) indicate in the PAR any subsequent transfer of the asset from one
accountable officer to another.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the
Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to properly account
for the stale checks in accordance with the provisions of Section 59 of NGAS Manual,
Volume I to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately avail the use of the ATM payroll
system in the payment of wages for the Municipal employees, including the Casual
and Job Order workers. This will protect not only the payroll funds from losses but
also the lives of the Accountable Officer/s who do withdrawals of huge amount of cash
from the bank.

Likewise, the use of such system will efficiently reduce the hours spent in
manually paying the wages of personnel.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Chairman and the LSB
members (a) continue to utilize the Special Education Fund for priority projects and
for purposes explicitly allowed in the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 1 and 2, s.
2020 in addition to Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; (b) be more specific in planning for the
PPAs to be implemented and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to address the
inconsistencies found in their reports and records.

We also recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant (a) observe the proper charging and reclassification of accounts in
accordance with the nature of transactions; and (b) make the necessary adjustment
and/or reclassification of accounts in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a)
ensure that qualifications of the BAC and TWG members are considered in
accordance with applicable laws and regulations; (b) direct the Accountant to
compute the payment of honoraria of procurement officials in accordance with
Budget Circular No. 2004-5A, as amended by BC No. 2007-2; (c) submit the required
documents mentioned in Item 5.7.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; and (d) ensure
that all future claims of honoraria are computed and granted in accordance with the
applicable laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
allow the BAC Chairperson to designate the members of the BAC-TWG in adherence
to Section 12.1 prescribed in the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
instruct the Municipal Engineer (a) in coordination with the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator, to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Projects
Status Accomplishment Report of all infrastructure projects, on-going and those that
are to be implemented during the year, to facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation
thereof; (b) strictly comply with the full disclosure policy of the government by
putting up the appropriate project signboards and notices on all the municipality’s
on-going projects and inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award and
before the start of the program/activity to validate the same for transparency and
accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a)
if possible, limit essential and regular duties of JOs in core offices to permanent and
casual employees, particularly those with accountability for government funds; (b)
require all department heads to carefully evaluate the performance of JOs and
consider those who perform well for regular positions; (c) require the JOs assigned
at the legislative department to use the biometrics for purposes of fairness; and (d)
submit accomplishment report on the job/tasks done in support of the payroll.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a)
facilitate the transfer of the inmates to the Municipal prison cell inasmuch as the
threat on the spread of COVID-19 has already been controlled; (b) request the PNP
Head at the Municipality to deploy the PNP personnel to the different Police Stations
within the Municipality; and (c) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to investigate
and/or look into the cause of high electric bills to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary
expenses.

We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed that the Local
Chief Executive (a) direct the Municipal Accountant and the various Department
Heads to coordinate and strictly observe the timely preparation and submission of the
Financial Reports, DVs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team within the
reglementary period prescribed under the aforementioned Circulars; (b) instruct the
Municipal Accountant to assess if additional workforce would be necessary to
facilitate the prompt submission of the documents to the Office of the Auditor.

We once again recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief
Executive (a) instruct the GFPS to set up, monitor and maintain appropriate systems
and mechanisms that would ensure the generation, processing, review, and updating
of sex-aggregated data or GAD database to serve as basis in the performance-based
and gender-responsive planning and budgeting; and (b) ensure critical assessment
and planning of GAD-related PPAs to allocate proper amounts that will help achieve
the set objectives whilst maximizing the use of the fund.

However, we recommended and Management agreed that the Local Executive
Chief instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the
General Ledger to update the book balances and carefully monitor the accounts to
reflect the correct amounts of withholding and remittances made to the BIR, GSIS,
PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG, thereby providing accurate information to readers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive Officer direct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) record the purchase of welfare goods for distribution in its inventory account and charge the expense account upon issuance to end-user
observations.managementAction: and (b) Municipal Services Officer to submit the utilization report/distribution list to COA for verification and validation purposes.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant (a) stop the granting of cash advances to elected officials other than for official travel; (b) grant cash advances only to duly designated disbursing officers who are properly bonded, pursuant to Items 3.2 and 4.5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and in accordance with the rules and regulations of the Bureau of Treasury; and (c) ensure timely liquidation of cash advances to accurately reflect the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal adheres to the audit recommendations. Effective April 1, 2021, the concerned department heads of the Municipality were designated by the Municipal Mayor as Special Disbursing Officers for expenditures or activities undertaken by their respective offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to correct all erroneous recording/classification noted for a fair presentation of the asset accounts in the financial statements. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive designate an Inventory Committee or Municipal General Services Officer whichever is applicable and require the (a) conduct complete annual physical inventory-taking of PPE (b) maintenance of complete property cards for property, plant and equipment which is to be reconciled with the records of the Municipal Accounting Office; and (c) notification to the Municipal Accounting Office upon the delivery of all purchased PPE and completion of infrastructure projects to ensure timely recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Inventory Committee was created to undertake the task of conducting a complete annual physical inventory-taking of PPE. To ensure maintenance of complete property records a General Services Section shall be created within the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and said records will be reconciled with the reports of the Accounting Department.
observations.reason: Not Implemented. No dedicated officer/personnel identified to date to take full charge of property and equipment management

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (a) exhaust possible means to secure copies of the TCT of the lots owned by the Municipality and submit to the Audit Team for verification
observations.managementAction: and (b) designate personnel to work on documenting the legality of ownership of the lots through a TCT in the name of the said lots. and to appropriate funds to cover the cost of such transfer of titles.
observations.reason: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to require the District Supervisors to submit the distribution list to support the recognition of expense of the Textbooks and Instructional Materials amounting to ₱1,965,658.50, and trace the Office Supplies difference of ₱202,284.89; (b) Municipal Treasurer henceforth, to document all deliveries of supplies and materials with the required IAR and with Requisition Issue Slip for the issuance thereof as well as with the distribution list for items categorized as for distribution; (c) Municipal Accountant to consistently record the purchased inventories to their respective asset accounts; and (d) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to ensure coordination between their offices for the reconciliation and timely recording of receipts and issuances of inventories.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant required the District Supervisor to submit the distribution list of the Textbooks and Instructional Materials The difference of ₱202,284.89 was traced and adjusted. The Municipal Treasurer still use the old format of government documents. The Management shall consistently record the purchased inventories to their respective asset accounts and ensure coordination between Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The Management still use the old format of government documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant recognize the Intra-Agency Receivables and Intra-Agency Payable accounts for the advances and receipt of funds from one fund to another for the correct presentation of the balances of the accounts affected and easily determine the actual amount involved.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was implemented and the necessary intra-agency accounts were recognized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict observance of the applicable regulation provided for the utilization of the fund it supports, such as in the case of LGSF-DRRAP and Local Budget Circular No. 134 which explicitly mandate that the project under the fund must be included in the Municipality’s LDRRM Plan and AIP, and the use of proper reporting format in accordance with the requirements of the applicable Budget Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality complied with the recommendation by strictly observing the applicable regulations on the proper use of externally sourced funds, as revision of the LDRRMFIP was done with the approval of the LCE and SB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) Local Development Council continue to plan projects that are capital in nature to attain desirable socio-economic development outcomes of the Fund
observations.managementAction: and (b) MENR Officer request for a separate budget for the garbage disposal tipping fees to refrain from charging inappropriate expenses under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) MDRRMC to (i) continue to monitor the implementation of all LDRRM PPAs to fully achieve the desired benefits therefrom; and (ii) prepare a revised AIP and LDRRMFIP to be approved by the Sangguniang Bayan and the LCE for all new projects to be funded from the DRRM unexpended balances and include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous year; and (b) Municipal Accountant to maintain corresponding Subsidiary Ledger for each type of Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMC shall furnish the Municipal Accounting Office a copy of the inventory of Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment to facilitate the proper classification and maintenance of subsidiary ledger for each type of equipment. The MDRRMC assured that new projects to be funded from previous years’ unexpended balances will be properly incorporated in a revised LDRRMFIP to be submitted to the LCE and Sangguniang Bayan for approval.
observations.reason: Not Implemented. This was not given due attention by the Management

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Chairman and the LSB members be more critical in the use of SEF and allocate the fund equitably and primarily amongst the public schools as intended by DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017.
observations.managementAction: The Management commented the following: • Salaries of JO employees did not exceed the appropriated amount. • As per the provincial Governor, Maintenance of the district vehicle and salary of the district driver shall be charged under SEF. • The IT Equipment were used by the Administrative Assistants and Officers during their work from home arrangement. • Electric Bills pertain to expenses of some select schools.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) evaluate the created MSWMB and identify individuals that could qualify to complete the composition of the Board; (b) direct the MSWMB to prioritize the completion of the 10-year MSWM Plan and ensure that the same be approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission within the year; and (c) plan and implement viable programs that will put emphasis on re-use, recycling and composting of wastes generated in the jurisdiction.
observations.managementAction: Due to the Enhanced Community Quarantine and other restrictions brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic, representatives from the private sector and even from non-municipal agencies were either no longer available to attend the MSWMB meetings. However, in February 2022, representatives from the Manufacturing Sector, Recycling Sector and Protection of Air and Water Quality Sector were nominated to be members of the MSWMB. The updated 10-year MSWM Plan will be finalized and the DENR-EMB shall schedule the NSWMC deliberation. Appropriate budget for the operational cost of running all the PPAs under the 10-year SWMP is yet to be finalized, the MENRO continues to be resourceful in carrying out its various projects aimed at protecting and conserving the environment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the GFPS to set up and maintain appropriate systems and mechanisms that would ensure the generation, processing, review, and updating of sex-aggregated data or GAD database to serve as basis in the performance-based and gender-responsive planning and budgeting; and (b) ensure critical assessment and planning of GAD-related PPAs to allocate proper amounts that will help achieve the set objectives whilst maximizing the use of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management adheres to the audit recommendations and noted the following: • The CBMS was not yet in place due to various reasons such as absence of funds, lack of equipment and personnel. • The GFPS, through MPDO and MSWDO maintains data using the System developed by DSWD Central Office. The System can generate data by barangay and can maintain updated statistics by applying population data of any given year. • The Municipal Community-Based Monitoring System (CBMS) Coordinating Board was established on March 21, 2022 under Executive Order No. 06-S-2022 to oversee the implementation of the CBMS operations in the Municipality.
observations.reason: The CBMS is yet to be fully implemented and the concerned departments shall exert effort to attain 100% implementation of their respective PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC Secretariat and the Municipal Accountant for the submission of the lacking documents and/or a satisfactory explanation by the concerned personnel on such other noted deficiencies for the evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The concerned departments/officials will submit the necessary lacking documents in the sampled infrastructure projects and goods, and to all projects to be implemented and goods to be procured this year onwards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to trace the balances which were carried forward in CY 2021 for the necessary adjustments in the General Ledger, update the book balances and carefully monitor the accounts to reflect the amounts of the actual withholding and remittances made to the BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG, thereby eliminating the false impression of indebtedness.
observations.managementAction: The tracing of balances which were carried forward in CY 2021 is on-going to prepare the necessary adjustments and update the book balances. In addition, the accounting personnel in charge of withholding and remittances were instructed to strictly monitor the accounts.
observations.reason: (See Finding No. 16)

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to enforce the settlement of disallowances within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed and enforced the settlement of disallowances within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal General Services Officer to prepare the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) as basis in recording the de-recognition from the books of the unserviceable properties carried in the PPE accounts and facilitate immediate disposal of the said unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 79 of P.D. 1445.

observations.managementAction: A separate Trust Fund Account is being maintained for the unexpended balance of 5% LDRRMF

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and  Management  agreed to require the planning officer to:

include in their plans and programs projects that have an impact on health and environment issues to control the spread of the virus;

provide additional allocation for PPAs for Covid-19 preventive measures in accordance with Item 3.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, the 20% DF; and

continue to be compliant in the preparation of COVID-19 related  PPAs and assure inclusion  thereof in the AIP in accordance with Section 2.1  DBM DILG JMC No. 02.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

request for authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts as provided for in COA Circular No. 2016-005; and

require the Municipal Accountant to carefully and diligently follow the provisions under the said COA Circular which gives the Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances and Fund Transfers.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Local School Board to:

follow the schedule of activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF budget as mandated in the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and

utilize the Special Education Fund for priority projects and for purposes explicitly allowed in the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 1 and 2, s. 2020 in addition to Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

The LSB also agreed to establish the needs-based criteria in allocating SEF among schools to ensure its efficient and effective use in accordance with its intended purpose.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

continue to complete NGA-funded projects in CY 2020 within their target deadlines to ensure enjoyment of benefits of target beneficiaries; and 

to spread the use of the government funds to more than one barangay in agreement with the main purpose of LGSF-AM which is to provide the needed fund in the delivery of basic needs of the municipalities in the implementation of their priority programs and projects as mentioned in General Appropriations Act (GAA) of FY 2020.
observations.managementAction:
The Local Access Road at Barangay San Antonio was completed on March 11, 2021.

No Local Government Support Fund – Assistance to Municipalities was released during CY 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We commended Management for its full compliance on the receipt and timely distribution of the subsidies to the identified eligible families within the Municipality. However, we recommended and Management agreed to: 

require the MSWD officer to submit the necessary documents attached to the payroll to support the receipt of subsidy; and




submit written justification on the delay in liquidation to DWSD RO III.
observations.managementAction: A Certification from the Municipal Accountant that the funds received from DSWD RO III for the 1st Tranche SAP has been fully liquidated and accounted for in the books as of July 30, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant require the Public Health Workers and the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer to submit proof of actual exposure to specific health hazards and occupational risks in accordance with DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 S. 2016 and DSWD Memorandum Circular 2013-015, respectively, in order to substantiate their monthly claim of Hazard Pay. 

We also recommended and Management agreed that the concerned offices prepare internal guidelines for approval to the Mayor of the specific criteria and standards on grant of Magna Carta Benefits to all entitled PHWs and PSW.

We recommended and Management ensured to:

continue to maintain the GAD database and use gender-monitoring tools like HGDG in compliance with JMC No. 2013-01; and

maximize the utilization of GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related PPAs in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided.

We commended the Management for procuring goods at lower prices during calamities and we recommended and Management ensured continued adherence with the rules of the Price Act Law, to set an example with other buyers and if possible, held the hoarders of goods liable for the violation of the said law.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant instruct the accounting staff concerned to:

maintain separate ledgers for premiums and various types of loans for the purpose of monitoring the contributions and loan payments to Pag-IBIG in an effort to accurately monitor the actual amounts due;

make sure that the amount withheld for a given month is remitted in full in the next month within the prescribed period; and

prepare correcting entries for the erroneous reclassification of ₱121,186.70 from Due to Pag-IBIG account to Accounts Payable to reflect the correct balance of the said accounts in the financial statements.
We recommended and the Management agreed to enforce the settlement of disallowances within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that Management stop granting cash advances to employees not designated as Special Disbursing Officer. 

We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer,

 (a) effect the timely liquidation of cash advances prior to granting the subsequent ones; 

(b) refrain from granting cash advances for payment of miscellaneous expenses with no specific and identified purpose; and 

(c) ensure that sufficient controls will be implemented in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Petty cash fund was set-up during the year for payment of various miscellaneous expenses.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local School Board of the Municipality of Bacolor 
(a) follow the schedule of activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF budget as mandated in the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and

 (b) continue to utilize the SEF to priority projects and only for purposes explicitly allowed in the Joint Circular.

We also recommended that the Accounting Office make the necessary adjustments on the improperly recorded transactions for the above-mentioned asset and expense accounts to properly monitor the utilization of the fund and for fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management

 (a) revisit the provisions stated under the Local Government Code, particularly on the passing of an ordinance for the collection of fees; 

(b)consider charging additional waste fees to the major business establishments that do not have their own hauling services; and 

(c) coordinate with the barangay, particularly those within the urban areas, to study the possibility of collecting waste fees from the households thru a barangay ordinance  and subsidize the Municipality from the fees collected as share for the payment of  tipping  fees so that the  fund could be utilized for other beneficial project of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to

 (a) address the issues that may have caused the delay in the approval of the LGUs SWM plans; 



(b) coordinate and work together with all its members and concerned officials to provide the systematic and thorough information entailed by the National Solid Waste Management Council and related provisions under R.A. No. 9003 for the definite approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan; and 

(c) ensure to strengthen the efficiency of solid waste management within its jurisdiction even prior to the approval of the plan.

We recommended that Management:

 (a) require the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate ledgers for each employee to prevent erroneous recording of loan deductions and ensure that remittances of loan payment will only be applied/posted to that specific agency; and 

(b) enroll the Municipality with Pag-IBIG’s online services for easier, faster, and more accurate reporting and remittance.

We recommended and Management agreed to (a) require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct a regular reconciliation of balances under the Cash in Bank account; (b) require the MT and other collecting officers to update their individual cashbooks regularly and indicate recapitulations/totals for each month for easier review;  (c) submit AFs and corresponding RCDs on the 10th day of the following month to COA for a timely review; and (d) exert best effort to validate and reconcile balances between cashbooks and ledgers, and make necessary adjustments once reconciling items have been verified and substantiated.

We recommended and Management agreed to (a) request for authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts as provided for in COA Circular No. 2016-005; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to carefully and diligently follow the provisions under the said COA Circular which gives the Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances and Fund Transfers.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require (a) the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in the recording of all Inventory Accounts to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials; (b) the MAO and MGSO to maintain the prescribed ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, for each type of Inventory,  keep track the actual Supplies Inventory on hand and reconcile the balances of the two inventory records.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Inventory Team to (i) conduct complete annual physical inventory-taking of PPE by type and submit a complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) not later than January 31 of each year; and (b) Municipal General Services Officer to maintain property cards for property, plant and equipment and effect its reconciliation with the ledger cards of the Municipal Accounting Office.

We also reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) investigate the discrepancy noted between the balances in the SL and schedules and effect the necessary adjustments to reduce it to minimum, if not zero out; (b) ensure that all government property officially received by the Municipality are accounted for and taken up in the books at acquisition cost or appraised value; (c) coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office with regards to the cost of partially completed infrastructure projects as of year-end and the breakdown of cost of each completed project lumped in one amount to ensure complete, accurate and timely recording thereof; (d) ensure monthly recognition of depreciation expense of all the PPE accounts; (e) make the necessary adjustments in the books to correct all  erroneous recording/classification noted;  and (f) adopt all the principles prescribed in PPSAS 17 for proper accounting of the Municipality’s PPE and for a fair presentation of the asset accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal General Services Officer to prepare the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) as basis in recording the de-recognition from the books of the unserviceable properties carried in the PPE accounts and facilitate immediate disposal of the said unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 79 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended and  Management agreed to (a) submit additional supporting documents for the conversion of Tinajero Elementary School--₱22,050.00, truck rental--₱14,600.00, and medicines--₱289,595.80; (b) submit an explanation for the incomplete information provided in the SDs for the purchase of groceries amounting to ₱59,400.00; (c) require the Municipal Accountant to verify completeness of attached SDs before payment is made; (d) require the concerned department to update the Municipality's website and make the necessary posting as part of their public disclosure requirements; and (e) return to the National Treasury the unutilized balance of ₱11,313.18 and the amount of ₱28,750.00, which is not among the allowable expenditures using the BGCM, after the lifting of State of Calamity.

We recommended  and Management agreed to (a) delegate the receipt and acknowledgement of the donations whether cash or in-kind to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office; (b) take heed of the basic controls in the receipt, recording, inventory, storage and distribution of all donations received even in the midst of a crisis; (c) submit the required reports together with the supporting documents; (d) require the Committee in charge of the Physical Inventory to include In-kind Donations received by the Municipality in the physical count and Inventory Report; (e) record the cash donations received under the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account as mandated by section IV.B.7 of COA Circular 2014-002; and (f) maintain a separate bank account with an authorized government depository bank (AGDB) where cash donations are to be deposited.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the (a) MDRRMC to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) indicating the (i) detailed PPAs and the corresponding appropriation classified as either Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) or Capital Outlay (CO); and (ii) under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years for ease of monitoring of project implementation and evaluation; (b) MDRRMC ensure that the LDRRMF be strictly utilized for the implementation of projects and activities for disaster prevention  and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery; (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) comply with the monthly submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF; (ii) and maintain corresponding Subsidiary Ledger for each type of Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment; and (iii) ensure that the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF is discussed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We also recommended and Management agreed that the amount irregularly charged against LDRRMF be restored to LDRMMF.

We recommended and  Management  agreed to require the planning officer to (a) include in their plans and programs projects that have an impact on health and environment issues to control the spread of the virus; (b) provide additional allocation for PPAs for Covid-19 preventive measures in accordance with Item 3.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, the 20% DF; and (c) continue to be compliant in the preparation of COVID-19 related  PPAs and assure inclusion  thereof in the AIP in accordance with Section 2.1  DBM DILG JMC No. 02.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Local School Board to (a) follow the schedule of activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF budget as mandated in the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and (b) utilize the Special Education Fund for priority projects and for purposes explicitly allowed in the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 1 and 2, s. 2020 in addition to Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) continue to complete NGA-funded projects in CY 2020 within their target deadlines to ensure enjoyment of benefits of target beneficiaries; and (b) to spread the use of the government funds to more than one barangay in agreement with the main purpose of LGSF-AM which is to provide the needed fund in the delivery of basic needs of the municipalities in the implementation of their priority programs and projects as mentioned in General Appropriations Act (GAA) of FY 2020.

We commended Management for its full compliance on the receipt and timely distribution of the subsidies to the identified eligible families within the Municipality. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant instruct the accounting staff concerned to (a) make sure that the amount withheld for a given month is remitted in full within the prescribed period; and (b) prepare correcting entries for the erroneous reclassification of ₱78,027.61from Due to PhilHealth account to Accounts Payable to reflect the correct balances of the said accounts in the financial statements.

 (a) investigate on the discrepancy noted and effect the necessary adjustments to reduce it to minimum, if not zero out;

We recommended that Management (a) require the job-order personnel who failed to present themselves  in our office to  personally report with their valid ID; (b)  require the JOs assigned at the legislative department to register and employ the mandatory use of the biometric system; (c) limit positions in core offices who perform the functions of regular employee; and (d) require all department heads to carefully evaluate or assess the need for a job order personnel with due consideration on their qualification to the job requirement.

We recommended that Management (a) assure that proper and adequate  planning on the utilization of resources is done and prioritize and review planned projects prior to implementation; (b) secure the building premises to protect it from possible destruction and loss caused by outside elements; (c) submit justification on the continuous non-usage of the structure; (d) schedule a meeting with officials concerned to determine which office or department is practically suited to stay at the said multi-purpose hall so that the property will be taken care of properly and further deterioration will be prevented.

(b) Local Chief Executive (LCE) and the Solid Waste Management Board (SWMB):

 (i) as soon as the budget allows, prioritize the creation of Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO); and 


(ii) impose the “no segregation no collection policy”, especially at the barangay level.

We also reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Management, through the SWMB: 

(a) consider developing  concrete programs and projects that are supportive of proper solid waste management and reduction of waste at source e.g. reuse, reduce and recycle of non-biodegradable materials; 

(b) conduct information and education campaigns on the hazards of open burning and strictly monitor compliance thereof especially at the barangay level; and 

(c) strengthen the information campaign by putting up signages in public places displaying the acts prohibited under Section 48 of R.A. No. 9003 and the corresponding sanctions thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan members are currently in the process of formulating ordinances supportive of the strategies and objectives of its 10-Year SWMP. Further, the Management and SWMB concurred with our recommendation and committed to develop concrete programs and projects for the enforcement of waste segregation and minimization at source and the proper disposal thereof.

Management agreed to our recommendation on the close monitoring of job order personnel especially on the proper recruitment and selection process in hiring thereof and in the periodic assessment of the need for job order personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) completely account for each item composing the PPE; (b) investigate on the discrepancy noted and effect the necessary adjustments; (c) make provisions for depreciation for all depreciable assets using the correct estimated useful life of assets; (d) ensure that all government property officially received by the Municipality are accounted for and taken up in the books at acquisition cost or appraised value; (e) reclassify CIP to its appropriate Asset account upon completion of the project; and (f) adopt all the principles prescribed in IPSAS 17 for proper accounting of the Municipality’s PPE and for a fair presentation of the asset accounts in the financial statements.
We also reiterated that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to handle the inventory and monitoring of PPE and ensure that mechanisms are in place to limit opportunities to misappropriate assets or conceal intentional misstatements in the financial statements and to avoid possible loss, theft and misuse of the Municipality’s assets and ensure the (a) regular conduct of physical count of all properties with the prescribed report to be submitted to COA on or before January 31 of each year; and (b) proper disposal of unserviceable assets in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the MTO to (a) submit the RCPI to the Auditor within the prescribed deadline; (b) consolidate weekly the RIS using the SSMI and submit to the Accounting office for recording of issued supplies; (c) coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office in reconciling their records of the Inventory on a per category basis; and (d) assign a separate full time employee to handle management of office supplies.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each type of Inventory and update their record in keeping track the actual Supplies Inventory on hand.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer (a) refrain from granting cash advances for payment of miscellaneous expenses with no specific and identified purpose; (b) effect the timely liquidation of cash advances prior to granting the subsequent ones; (c) set up a petty cash fund to pay for small value and non-recurring expenses; and (d) see to it that all applicable laws, rules and regulations affecting cash advances and disbursement of funds are strictly adhered to.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the GPBP focal persons to make use of lessons learned from the completed projects to improve efficiency in the implementation of similar NGA-funded projects in the future, especially in the implementation of the SLP and planning of more doable programs and projects;  (b) refund from the source agency the unutilized amount of the BUB fund, if the remaining identified programs and projects are no longer feasible/doable; and (c) aim to reduce further unnecessary delays in project implementation to allow the early enjoyment of benefits by the general public.

We recommended that Management (a) continue to implement projects which are capital or investment in nature and  implement projects not only those with economic development but also projects with desirable socio and environmental  development to fully attain the objective of the fund; (b) ensure full completion of the remaining projects identified in the AIP for CY 2018; (c) ensure that only feasible projects are included in the AIP; and (d) coordinate with the officials of the DPWH for their proposed projects within the Municipality and concerned Barangays so that possible duplication and overlapping will be readily determined and avoided.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) MDRRMC to prepare a comprehensive and detailed LDRRMFIP that will address risk reduction and management system effectively. It should be in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002 to meet the needs for prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery of the Local Government. PPAs shall be listed specifically and allotment for each should be properly classified as either MOOE or CO. It shall also be prepared in the format prescribed by the same Circular and shall include the details of the projects charged to the previous years’ unexpended balance of the DRRMF; (b) MDRRMO to strictly comply with the preparation of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in accordance with the format prescribed under COA Circular 2012-002, with emphasis on the utilization thereof and the inclusion of balances of previous years’ appropriation transferred to the Special Trust Fund, and submission of the to the Audit Team for timely verification thereof; (c) Municipal Accountant to follow the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 as regards the reporting requirements for the DRRMF, specifically the proper transfer of previous year’s unexpended balances of the DRRMF to the Trust Fund; and (d) Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for the DRRMF on the following: (i) RAAO - for Quick Response Fund; (ii) RAAO-for Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses; and (iii) RAAO- for Capital Outlay.

We recommended that the (a) Sangguniang Bayan, in coordination with the Solid Waste Management Board, formulate ordinances to carry out the requirements of RA 9003, specifically the imposition of fines and penalties to strengthen the implementation of solid waste management plan throughout the Municipality; (b) Local Chief Executive (LCE) and the Solid Waste Management Board (SWMB) (i) as soon as the budget allows, prioritize the creation of Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO); and (ii) strictly impose the “no segregation no collection policy”, especially at the barangay level; and (c) management consider augmenting the Budget allocated for environmental services and include urgent needs such as those related to the procurement of a new garbage truck and possible expansion and improvement of the MRF.

We recommended that the Local School Board of the Municipality of Bacolor (a) ensure full implementation of the programs, projects and activities funded under SEF; (b) effect the charging of ECCD-related programs to SEF so as not to deplete the budget of the GF; and (c) continue to utilize the SEF only for purposes explicitly allowed in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.
We also recommended that the Accounting Office make the necessary adjustments on the improperly recorded transactions for the above-mentioned expense accounts to properly monitor the utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the BAC to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the Local Chief Executive before the same is submitted to the GPPB either in printed or electronic format within the prescribed period; (b) instruct the Municipal Engineer to require from their contractors the issuance of warranty security on all completed projects that will cover the entire defects liability period; (c) require the GSO/MT to publish or post in public all PPAs being implemented during the year in conspicuous places within the Municipality or in the venue where  the said PPAs  are being carried out  for proper dissemination to the public; and (d) instruct the BAC to submit a copy of the contracts and purchase others within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, GSO/MT resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

We commended the effort of Management for surpassing targeted collections for various income accounts. We still recommended that the Local Finance Committee make a systematic and realistic forecasting to set-up attainable performance targets on all possible income accounts.

We recommended that management (a) in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer intensify tax collection efforts to generate additional income to finance projects beneficial to its constituents; (b) take initiatives to find ways and means in order to maximize the exercise of its revenue-raising power and adapt strategic means to improve collection efficiency; (c) through the Municipal Assessor initiate measures to further update values of property and coordinate with the Province of Pampanga regarding the required revision of accounts on real properties in the Municipality; (d) conduct tax mapping of businesses in a more frequent manner and implement strategies that would encourage business owners to pay their taxes; (e) communicate with the province on discrepancies noted in quarry collections; (f) conduct a study on the possible use of the RFID system in the quarry site and provide budget in the ensuing year and implement the same; and (g) take advantage of technology and avail of the BPLS offered by the province or any other MIS that would improve effectiveness and efficiency of the Municipality’s financial operations.

We recommended that management: (a) continue to improve its performance to further increase amount allocated for personal services and create plantilla positions for deserving and qualified personnel giving priority to those who have rendered service with the Municipality for a number of years; (b) strengthen its recruitment and selection process in hiring job order personnel by reviewing its current workforce, specific work to be done, and objectives before hiring personnel; (c) require the HR Officer to conduct the proper selection and recruitment process such as the conduct of written, oral, and medical examinations in order to ascertain applicant’s qualifications to all employees including those under the legislative department; and (d) require all department heads to carefully evaluate or assess the need for job order personnel with due consideration on their fitness to the job requirement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Technical Working Group to speed up the preparation of monitoring tools such as the HGDG and PIMME to strengthen the   implementation and evaluation of gender-related issues as required under JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that management (a) instruct the Budget Officer to allocate at least 1% of Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC); (b) submit accomplishment report to assess compliance of implementation on identified programs relating to child care protection and development; and (c) thru the LCPC, continue to implement programs, activities and projects that will  protect the interest   of children based on Annual Work and Financial Plan  as stated in Section 15 of R.A. No. 9344.
We recommended that management enforce the settlement of disallowances within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular 2009-006.

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the MTO to: 

(a) conduct physical count of all inventory items regularly at the end of every semester and render a report thereon within the prescribed deadline; and 

(b) reconcile the records of the MTO with the Accounting records on a per category basis.  

We further recommended that the LCE require the: 

(a) MTO to consolidate weekly the RIS using the SSMI and submit to the Accounting office for recording of issued supplies; and 

(b) Accounting office to follow the procedure of recording in the books the procurement of supplies inventory.

(b) consider enrolling accounts for various utilities under paper-less billing if possible.
observations.managementAction: The Management concurred with our recommendation. The Accounting Department assured the Audit Team, henceforward, that all expenses be recognized following the accrual basis of accounting.
The Management guaranteed that all ongoing projects planned for CY 2017 will be completed by the end of April 2018.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the City Treasurer(a) exercise prudence and due diligence in the processing of transactions; (b) require the disbursing officer to liquidate the cash advances within the prescribed period; (c) ensure that no cash advances are granted unless the previous ones are liquidated; (d) ensure completeness of signatures of approving officials before disbursements are made; and (e) conduct thorough review and evaluation of the affected dormant accounts and comply with all the documentary requirements and procedures under COA Circular 2016-005 for the proper disposition such as request for write-off and the disclosure of dormant accounts; and (f) see to it that all applicable laws, rules and regulations affecting cash advances and disbursement of funds are strictly adhered to.

We recommended that the Municipal Government, thru its Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor, initiate measures to increase collection of taxes through the conduct of tax campaign and implement civil remedies specified in the Local Government Code.

We reiterated the recommendation that the: (a) Municipal Administrator assign a non-accounting personnel to (i) handle the custody of office supplies inventory and the monitoring of issuance thereof to reduce the risks of undetected error and  opportunities for fraud; and (ii) conduct regularly the physical count of all properties with the prescribed report to be submitted to COA on or before January 31 of each year; and (b) Municipal Accountant  strictly comply with the accounting procedures prescribed in Section 114 of the NGAS Manual and ensure timely recording of supplies used.

We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Team to (a) comply with the regular conduct of physical count of all properties with the prescribed report to be submitted to COA on or before January 31 of each year; (b) give considerable time and additional personnel to work on the tagging or the labeling of all PPEs and assignment of property codes to ensure proper monitoring; and (c) allocate budget for the storage of medicines in the RHU and for offices in need of furniture and fixtures.
We also recommended that the (a) General Services Officer cause the proper disposal of unserviceable assets in accordance with applicable rules and regulations on disposal procedures; and (b) Municipal Accountant (i) ensure that all government property officially received by the Municipality are accounted for and recognized in the books at acquisition cost or at appraised value, as applicable; and (ii) maintain adequate subsidiary records for PPE so that relevant details are available to users thereof.

We reiterated our recommendation that the General Services Officer ensure that all insurable assets have insurance coverage with GSIS in order to be indemnified for the value of government properties lost or damaged in case of  theft, calamities  or any unforeseen events.

We recommended that  (a) Management limit the practice of serving meals and snacks during meetings particularly those events which are considered among the regular or normal activities of the Municipality; (b) Sangguniang Bayan Office limit their expenses for meals and observe prudence in utilizing government funds which are intended for public purpose; (c) Budget Office (i) ensure that expenses are charged to the appropriate account title; (ii) provide for an appropriation for Representation Expenses for meals and snacks served during meetings and conference instead of using Other Maintenance and Operating Expense and Miscellaneous Expense; and (d) Accounting Office record the expenses to the appropriate account title such that meals and snacks served for purposes other than meetings and conferences, or other than those under the category of representation expenses shall be recorded as Miscellaneous Expenses or Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses.

We recommended that the:(a) Municipal Accountant require the concerned official to submit additional documents to support the transactions chargeable against peace and order program; (b) Local Chief Executive observe the rules governing the proper utilization of Peace and Order fund; and(c) Budget Officer provide appropriation for Confidential Funds and in accordance with the rules governing the entitlement thereof per COA/DBM/DILG JMC No. 2015-01.

We recommended that the: (a) Local Chief Executive: (i) abide with the provisions of Civil Service Commission (CSC) Memorandum Circular No. 21 s. of 1991 and Rule XVII of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order (E.O.) 292 and ensure that all department heads enforce the policies as regards time and attendance system; (ii) consider upgrading the biometric system into a one which can directly generate the DTRs of individual employees following the format of CSC Form No. 48; and (iii) require the concerned employee for the submission of a written explanation or any proof of attendance with corresponding documents such as work accomplishments, otherwise immediately refund the amount of ₱61,043.82; (b) Department heads: (i) ensure that all employees under their supervision have a work schedule recorded in the timekeeping system; (ii) maintain a separate logbook in respective departments to record time ins and outs and leaves of employees and submit photocopies of these at the end of each pay period to the HRMO to serve as their check and balance; (iii) ensure that approved leave forms are submitted to the HRMO and a copy retained after submission thereto; (iv) review all DTRs for inconsistencies and ensure correctness of time and attendance of each employee before signing/approving such DTRs; (c) Human Resource Management Office: (i) prepare the daily time records of permanent employees and ensure that these were approved by their corresponding department heads before payment of salary is processed; (ii) consider requiring all employees to time in and out during lunch breaks to enforce employees to abide with the allowable break periods; (iii) consider installing an alternative mechanism/system for recording time and attendance of employees deployed in the field such as the use of logbook and ensure monitoring whether these employees abide with the timekeeping policies and are really rending actual services; (iv) develop and enforce concrete policies for those employees who fail to abide with the policies concerning time and attendance and filing of leaves of absences; and (d) Accounting and Treasury Department: (i) ensure completeness of required supporting documents, especially the proof of attendance for each employee, before processing payment of salary.

We recommended that the: (a) Local Chief Executive, or his authorized representative, require the OIC-MT of Sasmuan to submit to the Municipality of Bacolor DTRs or equivalent document duly approved by authorized official to  support the payment of salaries thereto; and (b) Municipal Administrator make a follow up with the Bureau of Local Government Finance Head Office regarding the confirmation of the Executive Director of BLGF for the designation and several extension of designations of the MT and reiterate the request for the designation of the AMT to  MT in the Municipality of Bacolor.

We recommended that the: (a) BAC, thru, its Secretariat, prepare the consolidated APP consistent with the Municipality’s duly approved annual appropriation and with approval of the Local Chief Executive for inclusion in the budget in the ensuing year; (b) Department heads be required to submit to the BAC Secretariat their individual PPMPs showing the detailed list of goods and infrastructure projects to be procured during the year. These PPMPs shall be evaluated and reviewed by the Municipal Budget Office  and duly consolidated by the BAC Secretariat into an Annual Procurement Plan to support the appropriated amount in the approved Annual Budget; and (c) BAC prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report and submit the same to the GPPB in the prescribed format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of every semester, as required under Section 12.2 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Government Procurement Act, to allow monitoring by appropriate government Oversight Bodies of compliance with the provisions of the Government Procurement Act and ensure transparency thereof.

We recommended that the Certificate of Acceptance be issued by the Municipal Mayor one (1) year after the Certificate of Completion or after the lapse of defects liability period and after all defects and failures, if any, have been repaired by the contractor and inspected by the Municipal Engineer. Furthermore, we recommended that the Performance Security of the Contractor be made to remain valid until the issuance by the Mayor of the final Certificate of Acceptance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) coordinate and cooperate closely with each other for the immediate submission of the Dec. 2016 disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents; and (b) henceforth, comply strictly with the submission of accounts and financial reports within the prescribed time.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) coordinate and cooperate closely with each other for the immediate submission of the Dec. 2016 disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents; and (b) henceforth, comply strictly with the submission of accounts and financial reports within the prescribed time.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant monitor the proper utilization and ensure that the fund be expended only to the activities enumerated in Section 1 of RA 5447, as amended/updated by the latest SEF utilization guidelines; (b) Municipal Accountant ensure accurate recording of SEF transactions; and (c) members of the LSB (i) maximize the utilization of SEF for priority projects as identified in the latest guidelines; and (ii) strictly comply with the guidelines provided in Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, series of 2017 dated January 19, 2017 in the preparation  of SEF Budget for CY 2018 and in the utilization of CY 2017 SEF Budget.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to: (a) carefully plan, identify and prioritize programs and projects that provide the long-term development benefits to its constituents; (b) group the PPAs of the 20% DF in accordance with its prescribed components; and (c) continue to implement the PPAs in a timely fashion and in accordance with the plans and targets for the year.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the DF be used only for expenditures which are capital in nature and not for operating and maintenance expenses.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) require the MSWD officer to closely monitor the utilization of SEA-K loans and  perform a random visit to beneficiaries; and (b) require the GPBP focal person of the respective implementing offices to (i) submit the accomplishment and monitoring reports and (ii) strictly monitor the implementation of projects as targeted and planned and take note of any gap for future inputs specially on the delay of implementation and completion of GPBP-funded projects and programs.

We recommended that the: (a) MSWDO: (i) exert best efforts for the immediate and full implementation of the Supplemental Feeding Program; (ii) revisit the provisions of IRR of RA 9184 particularly on the use of  alternative method of procurement thru community participation to expedite the procurements for the SFP; and (iii) submit the required supporting documents on subject disbursements such as photographs during the program and attendance list duly certified by the Day Care Worker In-charge, and coordinate with the BAC Secretariat to provide a copy of the failed biddings mentioned above; and (b) Accountant ensure the completeness of supporting documents before payment is made.

We recommended that the: (a) Sangguniang Bayan adopt the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2007-001 thru an Ordinance, specifically the prohibition of tricycle operation along national highways given the presence of  alternative routes; (b) Traffic Management Division formulate detailed information dissemination initiatives by employing specific signage or notices in conspicuous places on the prohibition of tricycle operation along national highways pending the formal adoption of the pertinent DILG Memorandum Circular on the matter; (c) Local Chief Executive consider the (i) deployment of an employee for the daily monitoring of the CCTV units to expand their benefits in preventing criminal incidents at the public market; and (ii) prioritization on the allocation of budget for CCTV units along major thoroughfares to monitor public safety as they are crucial in providing immediate rescue and assistance in emergencies from vehicle collisions, fire incidents and disasters.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person and the members of the Technical Working Group: (a) cause the preparation of the GAD database in coordination with the LPDO which will serve as basis for planning, implementing, and formulating GAD responsive activities; (b) use monitoring tools such as the HGDG and PIMME in monitoring and evaluating GAD activities; and (c) submit to the Audit Team the annual accomplishment report for evaluation.

We recommended that the: (a) Sangguniang Bayan in coordination with the Solid Waste Management Board legislate ordinances to carry out the requirements of R.A. No. 9003 so that efficient management of solid wasted will be effectively enforced and implemented; and (b) Local Chief Executive and the Solid Waste Management Board: (i) develop concrete programs and projects that are supportive of proper solid waste management and reduction of waste at source e.g. reuse, reduce and recycle of non-biodegradable materials; (ii) extend the collection of garbage at the barangay level; (iii) conduct information and education campaigns on the hazards of open burning and strictly monitor compliance thereof especially at the barangay level; and (iv) strengthen the information campaign by putting up signages in public places displaying the acts prohibited under Section 48 of RA 9003 and the corresponding sanctions thereof.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Accountant (a) invariably cause the timely recording of member’s premium and contribution and maintain subsidiary ledgers of the Due to GSIS account as required by the PPSAS such as the following
01 - Life and Retirement Premium
02 – ECC
03 - Salary Loan
04 - Policy Loan
(b) exert further efforts to reconcile employees’ records at GSIS to identify the affected employees on the unremitted accumulated balance of the Due to GSIS account; (c) check with the records whether all Personal Share (PS) deducted from the payroll of employees and Government Share (GS) and Employee’s Compensation Contribution (ECC) for all employees were remitted; and (d) cause the immediate remittance to GSIS for the unremitted premiums and contributions of employees.
